title: When You Think You’ve Had a Novel Idea: - Pause and Reflect: Ask, “have
subtitle: Spot Unintentional Plagiarism
description:
When you think you’ve had a novel idea:
- Pause and reflect: Ask, “Have I encountered this idea before?”
- Check sources: Revisit books, articles, or conversations you’ve recently engaged with.
- Document origins: Note down where your ideas come from to avoid confusion later.

Example: If you’re writing a report, double-check to ensure your “new” concept isn’t paraphrased from another source.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Pause and Reflect
howTo.summary: When you think you’ve had a novel idea, take a moment to pause. Ask yourself if you have encountered this idea before and consider its origins.

howTo.title: Conduct a Quick Search
howTo.summary: Perform a targeted search using key phrases from your idea. This helps identify any prior uses and ensures your concept is genuinely original.

howTo.title: Document Your Findings
howTo.summary: Log the sources and origins of your ideas in the Brali LifeOS app. This creates a clear record and helps avoid confusion later.

howTo.title: Verify and Validate
howTo.summary: If your idea will be used publicly, take extra time to verify its originality. Check multiple sources to ensure you are not unintentionally paraphrasing.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and maintain accountability. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/spot-unintentional-plagiarism/.
